seo_questions.bn: ঘরে আযান দেওয়া কি রোগ নিরাময় এবং সন্তান লাভে সহায়ক?
seo_questions.de: Kann das Rezitieren des Adhans zu Hause Krankheiten heilen und Kinder schenken?
seo_questions.en: What is the Islamic practice of reciting the adhan at home for healing and seeking children?
seo_questions.es: ¿Puede recitar el adhan en casa curar enfermedades y conceder hijos?
seo_questions.fa: آیا گفتن اذان در خانه برای شفا و فرزنددار شدن مؤثر است؟
seo_questions.fr: Réciter l'adhan à la maison peut-il guérir des maladies et accorder des enfants ?
seo_questions.id: Apakah mengumandangkan adzan di rumah dapat menyembuhkan penyakit dan mendatangkan keturunan?
seo_questions.ru: Помогает ли чтение азана дома вылечить болезни и обрести детей?
seo_questions.tr: Evde ezan okumak hastalıktan şifa ve çocuk sahibi olmaya yardımcı olur mu?
seo_questions.ur: کیا گھر میں اذان دینے سے بیماری اور بانجھ پن دور ہوتا ہے؟
seo_questions.zh: 在家中呼礼是否有助于治愈疾病和求得子嗣？

similar_content_hints.description: Hadiths about the spiritual and physical benefits of the adhan, including its use in warding off evil, illness, and bringing blessings into the household.
Traditions where the Imams prescribe specific acts of worship or supplication as remedies for medical conditions, including narrations about du'a for seeking children.
Narratives in which two or more companions independently confirm the efficacy of an Imam's prescription through their personal experience.

summaries.bn: এই হাদিসে ইমাম আবুল হাসান (আ.) দীর্ঘস্থায়ী রোগ ও বন্ধ্যাত্বের প্রতিকার হিসেবে ঘরে উঁচু গলায় আযান দেওয়ার নির্দেশ দিয়েছেন। হিশাম ইবনে ইব্রাহীম এই নির্দেশ পালনের পর অলৌকিক শিফা পেয়েছেন এবং মুহাম্মাদ ইবনে রাশিদ তাঁর নিজের ও পরিবারের সুস্থতার অভিজ্ঞতা বর্ণনা করে তা নিশ্চিত করেছেন।
summaries.de: Dieser Hadith berichtet, wie Imam Abu al-Hasan (a.s.) das laute Rezitieren des Adhans im Haus als Heilmittel für chronische Krankheit und Kinderlosigkeit vorschrieb. Hisham ibn Ibrahim erlebte nach Befolgung dieser Anweisung eine wunderbare Heilung, und Muhammad ibn Rashid bestätigt den Hadith durch seine eigene Erfahrung der Heilung für sich selbst und seine gesamte Familie.
summaries.en: This hadith recounts how Imam Abu al-Hasan (likely al-Ridha, AS) prescribed the practice of raising one's voice with the adhan inside the home as a remedy for chronic illness and childlessness. The narrator Hisham ibn Ibrahim experienced a miraculous cure after following this instruction, and Muhammad ibn Rashid corroborates the hadith by sharing his own parallel experience of healing for himself and his entire household. The hadith belongs to a section of al-Kafi dedicated to supplications and practices for seeking children, highlighting the Shia tradition of seeking blessings through specific acts of worship recommended by the Imams.
summaries.es: Este hadith relata cómo el Imam Abu al-Hasan (a.s.) prescribió elevar la voz con el adhan dentro del hogar como remedio para la enfermedad crónica y la infertilidad. Hisham ibn Ibrahim experimentó una curación milagrosa tras seguir esta instrucción, y Muhammad ibn Rashid corrobora el hadith compartiendo su propia experiencia de sanación para sí mismo y toda su familia.
summaries.fa: در این حدیث، امام ابوالحسن علیه السلام بلند کردن صدا به اذان در خانه را به عنوان درمان بیماری مزمن و نداشتن فرزند توصیه کرده‌اند. هشام بن ابراهیم پس از عمل به این دستور شفا یافت و محمد بن راشد نیز این تجربه را برای خود و خانواده‌اش تأیید کرد.
summaries.fr: Ce hadith relate comment l'Imam Abu al-Hasan (a.s.) a prescrit d'élever la voix avec l'adhan à l'intérieur du foyer comme remède à la maladie chronique et à l'absence d'enfants. Hisham ibn Ibrahim a connu une guérison miraculeuse après avoir suivi cette instruction, et Muhammad ibn Rashid corrobore le hadith en partageant sa propre expérience de guérison pour lui-même et toute sa famille.
summaries.id: Hadis ini menceritakan bagaimana Imam Abu al-Hasan (a.s.) meresepkan pratik mengumandangkan adzan di dalam rumah sebagai penawar penyakit kronis dan tidak memiliki keturunan. Hisham bin Ibrahim mengalami kesembuhan ajaib setelah mengikuti petunjuk ini, dan Muhammad bin Rashid membenarkannya dengan pengalaman pribadinya.
summaries.ru: В этом хадисе Имам Абу аль-Хасан (мир ему) предписал громкое чтение азана в доме в качестве средства от хронической болезни и бездетности. Хишам ибн Ибрахим пережил чудесное исцеление после следования этому указанию, а Мухаммад ибн Рашид подтверждает хадис, рассказывая о своём опыте исцеления себя и всей семьи.
summaries.tr: Bu hadiste İmam Ebu'l-Hasan (muhtemelen el-Rıza a.s.) kronik hastalık ve çocuksuzluk için evde yüksek sesle ezan okumayı tavsiye etmiştir. Hişam b. İbrahim buna uyduktan sonra mucizevi bir şifa yaşamış, Muhammed b. Rashid ise aynı uygulamayı yaparak hem kendi hem de ailesinin iyileştiğini teyit etmiştir.
summaries.ur: اس حدیث میں امام ابوالحسن علیہ السلام نے گھر میں بلند آواز سے اذان دینے کو دائمی بیماری اور اولاد نہ ہونے کا علاج بتایا۔ ہشام بن ابراہیم نے اس پر عمل کیا اور اللہ نے انہیں شفا دی۔ محمد بن راشد نے اپنا تجربہ بیان کرتے ہوئے اس کی تصدیق کی کہ اس عمل سے ان کے پورے گھر کی بیماریاں دور ہو گئیں۔
summaries.zh: 本哈迪斯记述了伊玛目阿布·哈桑（可能是第八位伊玛目里达，愿主赐他平安）将在家中大声呼礼作为治疗慢性病和不育的方法加以推荐。希沙姆·本·易卜拉欣遵循这一指示后获得奇迹般的痊愈，穆罕默德·本·拉希德则以亲身经历证实了这一做法对其本人及全家的疗效。

translation.ur: تو اس نے اسے حکم دیا

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/18)

parts.text: عَلِيِّ بْنِ مَهْزِيَارَعَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ رَاشِدٍ

text: هُ شَكَا إِلَى أَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) سُقْمَهُ وَ أَنَّهُ لَا يُولَدُ لَهُ فَأَمَرَهُ أَنْ يَرْفَعَ صَوْتَهُ بِالْأَذَانِ فِي مَنْزِلِهِ
قَالَ فَفَعَلْتُ فَأَذْهَبَ اللَّهُ عَنِّي سُقْمِي وَ كَثُرَ وُلْدِي

قَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ رَاشِدٍ وَ كُنْتُ دَائِمَ الْعِلَّةِ مَا أَنْفَكُّ مِنْهَا فِي نَفْسِي وَ جَمَاعَةِ خَدَمِي وَ عِيَالِي حَتَّى إِنِّي كُنْتُ أَبْقَى وَحْدِي وَ مَا لِي أَحَدٌ يَخْدُمُنِي فَلَمَّا سَمِعْتُ ذَلِكَ مِنْ هِشَامٍ عَمِلْتُ بِهِ فَأَذْهَبَ اللَّهُ عَنِّي وَ عَنْ عِيَالِيَ الْعِلَلَ وَ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Abbas Bin Marouf, from Ali Bin Mahziyar, from Muhammad Bin Rashid who said,

‘Hisham Bin Ibrahim narrated to me that he complained to Abu Al-Hassanasws of his illness and there were no children for him. So heasws ordered him to raise his voice with the Call to the Prayer (Azaan) in his house.
He said, ‘So I did it, and Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Caused my illness to go away from me, and I had abundant children’.

Muhammad Bin Rashid (the narrator) said, ‘I used to be ill all the time, being dejected from it with regards to myself, and (also) a group of my attendants, and my family members to the extent that I used to remain alone, and there would be no one to attend to me. So when I heard that from Hisham, I acted by it. So Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Caused the illness to go away from me, and from my family members. And the Praise is due to Allah<sup>azwj</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 9. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-‘ Abbas ibn Ma’ruf from Ali ibn Mahziyar from Muhammad ibn Rashid who has said the following: “Hisham ibn Ibrahim narrated to me that he complained before abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about not having a child. He (the Imam) commanded him to raise his voice with Adhan in his house. He has said, ‘I followed the instruction: then Allah removed my shortcoming and my children became many.’ Muhammad ibn Rashid has said, ’I remained ill all the time as well as a group of my servants and family (wife), so I would remain alone at home without anyone to help. I heard it from Hisham, I then practiced it and Allah removed the illnesses from me and my family. All praise belongs to Allah.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/19)
text: قَالَ لَهُ رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ خُرَاسَانَ بِالرَّبَذَةِ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ لَمْ أُرْزَقْ وَلَداً فَقَالَ لَهُ إِذَا رَجَعْتَ إِلَى بِلَادِكَ وَ أَرَدْتَ أَنْ تَأْتِيَ أَهْلَكَ فَاقْرَأْ إِذَا أَرَدْتَ ذَلِكَ وَ ذَا النُّونِ إِذْ ذَهَبَ مُغاضِباً فَظَنَّ أَنْ لَنْ نَقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ فَنادى فِي الظُّلُماتِ أَنْ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ سُبْحانَكَ إِنِّي كُنْتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ إِلَى ثَلَاثِ آيَاتٍ فَإِنَّكَ سَتُرْزَقُ وَلَداً إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad Al Asimy, from Ali Bin Al Hassan Al Taymuli, from Amro Bin Usman, from Abu Jameela,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws, said, ‘A man from the people of Khurasan said to himasws, May I be sacrificed for youasws! I have not been graced with children’. So heasws said to him: ‘When you return to your city and intend to go to your wife, so recite whenever you intend that, ‘[21:87] And the one with the whale (Yunus), when he went away in anger, so he thought that We had no Power over him, so he called out in the darkness: There is no god but You, Glory be to You; surely I am from the unjust ones – up to three Verses, so you would be Graced with a child, Allahazwj Willing’.
en.sarwar: 10. Ahmad ibn Muhammad al-‘Asemiy has narrated from Ali ibn al-Hassan al-Taymuliy from ‘Amr ibn ‘Uthman from abu Jamilah who has said the following: “Once, a man from Khurasan in al-Rabadhah said to abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘I pray to Allah to keep my soul in service for your cause, I have not been granted any children.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘When you return to your country and then decide to go to bed with your wife then read, ‘When the companion of fish went away in anger he thought that We will not apply any measures against him. He then in the dark called, “I testify that no one other than You deserves worship. You are free of all defects. I have been of those who wrong themselves.” Read up to three verses, you will be granted a child by the will of Allah.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/19)
text: لَمْ يُولَدْ لِي شَيْ‏ءٌ قَطُّ وَ خَرَجْتُ إِلَى مَكَّةَ وَ مَا لِي وَلَدٌ فَلَقِيَنِي إِنْسَانٌ فَبَشَّرَنِي بِغُلَامٍ فَمَضَيْتُ وَ دَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) بِالْمَدِينَةِ فَلَمَّا صِرْتُ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ قَالَ لِي كَيْفَ أَنْتَ وَ كَيْفَ وَلَدُكَ فَقُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ خَرَجْتُ وَ مَا لِي وَلَدٌ فَلَقِيَنِي جَارٌ لِي فَقَالَ لِي قَدْ وُلِدَ لَكَ غُلَامٌ فَتَبَسَّمَ ثُمَّ قَالَ سَمَّيْتَهُ قُلْتُ لَا قَالَ سَمِّهِ عَلِيّاً فَإِنَّ أَبِي كَانَ إِذَا أَبْطَأَتْ عَلَيْهِ جَارِيَةٌ مِنْ جَوَارِيهِ قَالَ لَهَا يَا فُلَانَةُ انْوِي عَلِيّاً فَلَا تَلْبَثُ أَنْ تَحْمِلَ فَتَلِدَ غُلَاماً .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Musa Bin Ja’far, from Amro Bin Saeed, from Muhammad Bin Amro who said,

‘There were no births for me at all, and I went out to Makkah and there was no child for me. So a person met me and gave me the good news of a boy. So I passed by and went to Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> at Al-Medina. So when I came to be in front of him<sup>asws</sup>, he<sup>asws</sup> said to me: ‘How are you, and how is your son?’ So I said, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! I went out and there was no child for me. So a neighbour of mine met me, so he said to me, ‘There has been born a boy for you’. So he<sup>asws</sup> smiled, then said, ‘Have you named him yet?’ I said, ‘No’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Name him Ali, for my<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup>, whenever there was a delay by a slave girl from his<sup>asws</sup> slave girls over it, said to her: ‘O so and so! Make the intention (of naming him) Ali’. So it would not be long before she would become pregnant and give birth to a boy’.
en.sarwar: 11. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Musa ibn Ja‘far from ‘Amr ibn Sa‘id from Muhammad ibn ‘Umar [‘Amr] who has said the following: “I never had a child . I went to Makkah and did not have any children. A man met me and gave good news of the birth of a boy to me. I then went on and visited Ali ibn al-Husayn, ‘Alayhi al-Salam. When I went before him (the Imam), he (the Imam) asked, ‘How are you and how is your son?’ I said, ‘I pray to Allah to keep my soul in service for your cause, I left home but I did not have a son. My neighbor met me and said, “A boy is born to you .’” He (the Imam) smiled and asked, ‘Have you given him a name?’ I replied, ‘No, I have not given him a name.’ He (the Imam) said, ‘Name him Ali; whenever a delay would take place in giving birth of any of my father’s girls he would ask her to keep her intention to name him Ali, then without much delay she would give birth to a boy.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/19)
text: إِذَا أَرَدْتَ الْوَلَدَ فَقُلْ عِنْدَ الْجِمَاعِ اللَّهُمَّ ارْزُقْنِي وَلَداً وَ اجْعَلْهُ تَقِيّاً لَيْسَ فِي خَلْقِهِ زِيَادَةٌ وَ لَا نُقْصَانٌ وَ اجْعَلْ عَاقِبَتَهُ إِلَى خَيْرٍ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moala Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali, from Aban Bin Usman, from Hareyz, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Whenever you want the child, so say during copulation, ‘O Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! Grant me a child and Make him to be pious, neither there being in his creation any excess nor a deficiency, and Make his posterity to be good’.
en.sarwar: 12. Al-Husayn from Muhammad has narrated from Mu‘alla’ ibn Muhammad from al-Hassan ibn Ali from Aban ibn ‘Uthman from Hariz from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘When you want to have a son then at the time of your going to bed with your wife say, “O Lord, grant me a son, make him pious, without any deficiency or extra in his physical condition and make his consequences in goodness.’”
